description: 48F, dyslipidemia, LDL 155, ASCVD 11%, no diabetes, no ASCVD. ACC/AHA primary prevention R4 (COR I, LOE A) preempts USPSTF Grade B (P3). Tests the higher-risk end of the preemption spectrum — previous fixtures only tested P4 (Grade C preemption).

actions.label: Initiate moderate-to-high-intensity statin therapy for primary prevention
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA R4 (COR I, LOE A): adults 40-75 without diabetes, LDL 70-189, ASCVD risk ≥7.5% should initiate moderate-to-high-intensity statin. LDL 155 is within the 70-189 range. ASCVD risk 11% (≥10%) strengthens the case for high-intensity. USPSTF Grade B also matched (ASCVD ≥10%, ≥1 risk factor: dyslipidemia) but is preempted by the more specific ACC/AHA guidance (P3, ADR 0018).

actions.label: Monitor lipid panel response to statin therapy
actions.rationale: Follow-up lipid panel 4-12 weeks after statin initiation to assess LDL-C response. ACC/AHA recommends ≥30% LDL-C reduction on moderate-intensity, ≥50% on high-intensity.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend statin based solely on USPSTF Grade B guidance
contraindications.rationale: USPSTF Grade B is preempted by ACC/AHA primary prevention rec (P3, ADR 0018). ACC/AHA provides LDL-stratified guidance and intensity recommendations that USPSTF lacks. Recommending under USPSTF alone yields only moderate-intensity; ACC/AHA's risk-stratified approach may warrant high-intensity at ASCVD 11%.
